title: calathea rubribracteata
intro: Calathea rubribracteata, also known as the Peacock Calathea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. The plant's leaves are known for their striking colors and patterns, and it is often grown as an ornamental plant. The leaves and roots of Calathea rubribracteata have been used in traditional medicine, and they are said to have medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves can be made into herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing stress and improving digestion.
